name: soulbot_execute_engine
description: SoulBot Execute Engine v5.17.0 -- MINOR: Sovereignty Bug Protocol. A1 sovereignty_bug_protocol shared section (main.aisop.json + agent_engine.aisop.json): sovereignty_toolset enumeration, self_modification_red_line, bug_classification 3-tier, bug_legal_channel (separation of powers), exposure_chain_spec (sovereignty_log prev_hash chain). B1 RULES.sovereignty_bug execution rule in agent_engine (Critical=WAITING_USER, Medium=report, Light=summary). X3 sovereignty enforcement retained. HMAC Phase 2, deterministic dispatch, server-side agent_id. 4 modules, 14 nodes. 0 breaking changes.

modules.role: Engine Router -- intent matching, engine selection, execution orchestration, WAITING_USER/MESSAGE_PENDING interrupt relay

Node Engine -- node-by-node execution with Sub Agent dispatch, crash recovery, circuit breaker, parallel dispatch, deterministic dispatch_plan generation, dispatch_audit, bootstrap integrity enforcement (B1), user_gate NODE VERIFY (B2), user_gates_presented signal (B4)

Normal Engine -- node-by-node inline execution for loading_mode=normal packages, sys.* inline handling, NodeVerify, sovereignty gate enforcement (B1, v5.16.0 — writeCache.step3 runs user_gate_audit.py --enforce for terminal inline nodes)

Agent Engine -- Sub Agent execution contract with sovereignty gate precedence (B3), agent-mode last_completed_node advancement (B1, v5.15.0), and RULES.sovereignty_bug (v5.17.0 — 3-tier classification for sovereignty toolset bugs). init, execute with sys.* RULES + sovereignty_bug, review, writeCache with steps_done tracking

limitations: Router does not read target AIAP content -- only passes path to Engine
Normal Engine crash recovery (NM4) is simplified compared to Node Engine -- no parallel dispatch (half-open circuit breaker added in v5.2.0, orphan functions cleaned in v5.3.0 C3)
Parallel dispatch only available in Node Engine for independent mermaid branches
sys.io.confirm/input/select are forced-blocking -- user sovereignty preserved (Axiom 0)
Decision Node gate routing requires both mermaid diamond syntax AND cache route field
generate_dispatch_plan.py handles mode assignment only; parallel_waves analysis stays with orchestrator (mermaid parsing not migrated this cycle)

safety_card.user_input_points: All sys.io.confirm/input/select calls are forced_blocking -- user sovereignty preserved. Natural language USER_INPUT trigger words also force blocking.
safety_card.error_handling: retry with exponential backoff -> circuit breaker -> fallback -> inform user. on_error routing for typed errors (assertion_error, command_error, confirm_rejected, etc.)

eu_ai_act_art50.applicability: APPLICABLE — SoulBot Execute Engine orchestrates AI agent interactions with users. Art.50(1) requires AI-generated output marking. Art.50(2) requires disclosure when users interact with AI.
compliance_measures: output_mode includes explicit AI-generated disclosure prefix (Art.50(1) transparency labelling)
All responses begin with AI-signifying emoji prefix (visual disclosure)
system_prompt declares AI agent identity (Art.50(2) interaction disclosure)
safety_card documents compliance status for audit trail
Axiom 0 forced-blocking preserves human sovereignty (Art.50 spirit)
Plausible interaction standard applied: disclosure triggered where AI interaction is 'plausible' not just 'certain', per Commission draft Art.50 guidelines interpretation for autonomous agents
eu_ai_act_art50.watermarking_status: NOT_REQUIRED — produces structured orchestration output, not synthetic media. Art.50(4) watermarking applies to image/audio/video.
eu_ai_act_art50.penalties_context: Up to 35M EUR or 7% global turnover for non-compliance
eu_ai_act_art50.current_mitigations: Axiom 0 forced-blocking, output_mode transparency disclosure, trust_level=3, safety_card audit trail

frameworks.name: OWASP ASI (Agentic Security Initiative) 2026
frameworks.status_summary: 6 GREEN + 4 YELLOW->GREEN (ASI01, ASI06, ASI07, ASI10 addressed in v5.3.0 B1)

frameworks.name: NIST CAISI (AI Agent Standards Initiative) 2026
frameworks.coverage: Three-pillar alignment: standards facilitation (AIAP protocol), agent security/identity (server-side agent_id, dispatch_records), open-source protocol (AISOP specification)
frameworks.status_summary: MONITORING — AI Agent Interoperability Profile planned Q4 2026. NCCoE agent identity concepts (OAuth/OIDC/SPIFFE) tracked for future adoption.

mitigations.ASI06_cache_integrity: cache HMAC verification on _index.json and node caches
mitigations.ASI07_ipc_signatures: inter-agent cache IPC signatures via span_id correlation
mitigations.ASI10_runtime_drift: version field comparison across modules at bootstrap
mitigations.ASI06_key_rotation: HMAC key rotation procedure: (1) generate new HMAC key via secrets.token_bytes(32), (2) enable dual-key window — accept both old and new keys for verification (grace period: 24h recommended), (3) verify new key acceptance across all cache read paths, (4) disable old key after grace period confirmation. Rotation interval: 90-day recommended, emergency rotation on key compromise. All verification MUST use hmac.compare_digest() (constant-time, ref CVE-2026-21713).

annex_a_controls.A.6_data_management: execution_cache scoped, atomic writes, HMAC Phase 1
annex_a_controls.A.8_transparency: EU AI Act Art.50 disclosure compliance

quality_summary.note: v5.17.0 FINALIZED by ReviewFinalize (cache/62). Full scope (4/4 modules). ThreeDimTest: C=4.930 I=4.950 D=4.900 weighted=4.930 Grade S. governance_hash sha256:0d1f81c467b9. Previous: v5.16.0 final_adjusted=4.930 Grade S.
